[triangleOffset, setTriangleOffset] = useState(0);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!desktopMenu || !navRef.current) return;\n    const nav = navRef.current;\n    const triggerEl = desktopMenu === \"services\" ? servicesTriggerRef.current : resourcesTriggerRef.current;\n    if (!triggerEl) return;\n    const navCenter = nav.offsetWidth / 2;\n    const triggerCenter = triggerEl.offsetLeft + triggerEl.offsetWidth / 2;\n    setTriangleOffset(triggerCenter - navCenter);\n  }, [desktopMenu]);\n\n  return (\n    <div className=\"hidden xl:flex items-center\">\n      <nav\n        ref={navRef}\n        className=\"relative inline-flex items-center gap-1 p-1.5 rounded-full border border-border-soft overflow-visible\"\n        aria-label=\"Primary navigation\"\n        onMouseLeave={() => setDesktopMenu(null)}\n        onBlur={onDropdownBlur}\n      >\n        {/* Left links (Home, About) */}\n        {leftLinks.map((link) => (\n          <a\n            key={link.href}\n            href={link.href}\n            aria-current={isActive(link.href) ? \"page\" : undefined}\n            onClick={closeAllMenus}\n            className={cn(navLinkBase, isActive(link.href) && navLinkActive)}\n          >\n            {link.label}\n          </a>\n        ))}\n\n        {/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n         * SERVICES centering. */}\n        <div\n          ref={servicesTriggerRef}\n          onMouseEnter={() => setDesktopMenu(\"services\")}\n          onFocusCapture={() => setDesktopMenu(\"services\")}\n        >\n          <a\n            href={routes.services}\n            aria-expanded={desktopMenu === \"services\"}\n            aria-controls={servicesMenuId}\n            aria-current={servicesActive ? \"page\" : undefined}\n            onClick={closeAllMenus}\n            className={cn(navLinkBase, servicesActive && navLinkActive)}\n          >\n            Services\n            <ChevronDown\n              className={cn(\n                \"ml-1 w-3.5 h-3.5 transition-transform duration-200\",\n                desktopMenu === \"services\" && \"rotate-180\"\n              )}\n              aria-hidden=\"true\"\n            />\n          </a>\n        </div>\n\n        {/* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n         * RESOURCES TRIGGER\n         * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */}\n        <div\n          ref={resourcesTriggerRef}\n          onMouseEnter={() => setDesktopMenu(\"resources\")}\n          onFocusCapture={() => setDesktopMenu(\"resources\")}\n        >\n          <a\n            href={routes.resources}\n            aria-expanded={desktopMenu === \"resources\"}\n            aria-controls={resourcesMenuId}\n            aria-current={resourcesActive ? \"page\" : undefined}\n            onClick={closeAllMenus}\n            className={cn(navLinkBase, resourcesActive && desktopMenu === \"resources\" && \"rotate-180\"\n              )}\n              aria-hidden=\"true\"\n            />\n          </a>\n        </div>\n\n        {/* Right links (Career, Contact) */}\n Current theme is ${theme}.`}\n    >\n      {isDark ? (\n        <Sun className=\"w-[16px] h-[16px] transition-opacity\" aria-hidden=\"true\" />\n      ) : (\n        <Moon className=\"w-[16px] h-[16px] transition-opacity\" aria-hidden=\"true\" />\n      )}\n    </button>\n  );\n}\n","import { useState, type RefObject } from \"react\";\nimport { ChevronDown, Phone } from \"lucide-react\";\nimport type { MenuColumn, MenuItem } from \"@/exxonim/components/navigation/types\";\nimport { Button } from \"@/exxonim/components/primitives/Button\";\nimport { routes } from \"@/exxonim/routes\";\nimport { cn } from \"@/exxonim/utils/cn\";\nimport { ThemeToggle } from \"@/exxonim/components/navigation/ThemeToggle\";\nimport type { Theme } from \"@/exxonim/types\";\n\n/**\n * Mobile navigation panel — expanding section inside the floating pill.\n *\n * MOBIN-INSPIRED PATTERN:\n *   Instead of a separate overlay or side drawer, this panel is rendered\n *   INSIDE the pill's <header>.  ── separator ──\n *   [Services ▼ accordion]\n *   [Resources ▼ accordion]\n *   ── separator ──\n *   [● Track Consultation] (highlighted)\n *   [Theme Toggle]\n *   [Call Now CTA]\n *\n * Active\n            </span>\n          )}\n        </span>\n        <ChevronDown\n          className={cn(\n            \"w-3.5 h-3.5 text-accent transition-transform duration-300\",\n            open open ? \"grid-rows-[1fr] opacity-100\" : \"grid-rows-[0fr] opacity-0\"\n        )}\n      >\n        <div className=\"overflow-hidden\">\n          <div className=\"px-4 pb-3 pt-0.5 grid gap-3\">\n         size=\"compact\"\n                variant=\"primary\"\n                href={ctaHref}\n                onClick={onClose}\n              >\n                {ctaLabel}\n              </Button>\n              <a\n                href={secondaryHref}\n                onClick={onClose}\n                className=\"text-[12px] font-medium text-accent hover:underline\"\n              >\n                {secondaryLabel}\n              </a>\n            </div>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n      </div>\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n\nexport function MobileNavigationPanel({\n  brandName,\n isActive,\n  onClose,\n  theme,\n  onToggleTheme,\n}: MobileNavigationPanelProps) {\n  return (\n    <div\n      id={id}\n      aria-hidden={!isOpen}\n      className={cn(\n        \"grid *\n * BACKEND / ADMIN INTEGRATION NOTES (FastAPI + PostgreSQL):\n * ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n * Navigation is HARDCODED and does NOT require API calls.\n * To add, remove, or reorder navigation items, edit this file and redeploy.\n *\n * In a future phase, if the admin panel needs to manage navigation dynamically,\n * the FastAPI backend should expose a GET /navigation endpoint that returns a\n * structure matching the types used here (MenuItem, MenuColumn, FeatureBox),\n * and the Navigation component can be updated to fetch from that API with this\n * config as the fallback. See the deprecated navigationService.ts for the\n * previous API integration pattern.\n *\n * Guidelines for nav structure:\n * ─────────────────────────────\n * - Max 4–5 regular links (leftLinks + rightLinks combined) to avoid overflow\n *   on desktop. The nav pill uses `hidden xl:flex` so it only shows on ≥1280px.\n * - Max 2 dropdown groups per menu (Services, Resources).\n * - Max 5 items per dropdown group (keeps dropdowns compact).\n * - Dropdown item `href` values should point to valid routes defined in routes.ts\n *   OR be valid external URLs (https://...).\n * - The `highlightLink` is for the primary differentiator service. It renders\n *   with a distinctive accent-colored pill and animated indicator dot so it\n *   stands out from regular links.\n * - When adding a new page route, also update:\n *     1. routes.ts — add the route constant\n *     2. src/exxonim/pages/ — create the page component\n *     3. src/exxonim/app/App.tsx — add the static route entry\n *   The admin UI should warn that new routes require a code deploy.\n *\n * MEGA MENU LAYOUT:\n * ─────────────────\n * Each dropdown supports two layout variants:\n *   - \"split\"  → 50/50 two-column grid, both sides contain link items\n *   - \"feature\" → 60/40 grid, left side has items, right side has a\n *                 solid contrasting feature box (CTA card)\n *\n * DYNAMIC FEATURE BOX (Resources):\n * ────────────────────────────────\n * The Resources dropdown has a feature box that changes content based on\n * which menu item is hovered. The hoverFeatureMap maps each item's href\n * to a FeatureBox configuration. When no item is hovered, the default\n * resourcesFeatureBox is shown.\n *\n * BACKEND: To make menu content dynamic, create a `navigation_menus` table:\n *   TABLE navigation_menus (\n *     id            SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,\n *     key           VARCHAR(50) UNIQUE NOT NULL,  -- 'services' | 'resources'\n *     layout        VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'split',  -- 'split' | 'feature'\n *     columns       JSONB NOT NULL,       -- Array of MenuColumn objects\n *     feature_box   JSONB,                -- FeatureBox object or NULL\n *     updated_at    TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()\n *   );\n */\n\nimport { routes } from \"@/exxonim/routes\";\nimport type { FeatureBox, HoverFeatureMap, MegaMenuLayout, MenuColumn, MenuFooterCta, MenuItem } from \"@/exxonim/components/navigation/types\";\n\nexport const staticNav = {\n  /** Links rendered before the dropdown menus (left side of nav pill) */\n  leftLinks: [\n    { label: \"Home\", href: routes.home },\n    { label: \"About\", href: routes.about },\n  ] as MenuItem[],\n\n  /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   * SERVICES MEGA MENU — Split layout (50/50)\n   * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   *\n   * BACKEND: These reflect Exxonim's 5 core service areas:\n   *   1. icons)\n   */\n  servicesLayout: \"split\" as MegaMenuLayout,\n  servicesColumns: [\n    {\n      title: \"Registration & Setup\",\n      items: [\n        {\n          label: \"Entity        label: \"Work Permits\",\n          href: routes.services,\n          description: \"Foreign investor and work permit processing\",\n          icon: \"passport\" as const,\n        },\n      ],\n    },\n    {\n      title: \"Compliance & Operations\",\n      borderLeft: true,\n      items: [\n        {\n          label: \"Statutory Filings\",\n          href: routes.services,\n          description: \"Recurring filing obligations on schedule\",\n          icon: \"clipboard-list\" as const,\n        },\n        {\n          label: \"Regulatory Renewals\",\n          href: routes.services,\n          description: \"Proactive tracking and timely renewals\",\n          icon: \"refresh\" as const,\n        },\n        {\n          label: \"Operational Advisory\",\n          href: routes.services,\n          description: \"Structured guidance for managing obligations\",\n          icon: \"compass\" as const,\n        },\n      ],\n    },\n  ] as MenuColumn[],\n  servicesFeatureBox: undefined as FeatureBox | undefined,\n\n  /* ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   * RESOURCES MEGA MENU — Feature layout (60/40)\n   * ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════\n   *\n   * Left side (7 cols): Two sections of links — \"Learn\" and \"Get Help\"\n   *   - NO icons on items (clean, minimal)\n   *   - Items trigger dynamic feature box on hover\n   * Right side (5 cols): Feature box CTA card\n   *   - Content changes based on which item is hovered\n   *   - Default: \"Latest insights\" (Blog)\n   * Below feature box: Footer CTA row (thin, no border)\n   */\n  resourcesLayout: \"feature\" as MegaMenuLayout,\n  resourcesColumns: [\n    {\n      title: \"Learn\",\n      items: [\n        {\n          label: \"Blog\",\n          href: routes.blog,\n          description: \"Practical guides and compliance updates\",\n        },\n        {\n          label: \"FAQ\",\n          href: routes.faq,\n          description: \"Common registration and compliance questions\",\n        },\n      ],\n    },\n    {\n      title: \"Get Help\",\n      borderLeft: true,\n      items: [\n        {\n          label: \"Support\",\n          href: routes.support,\n          description: \"Get help from the Exxonim team\",\n        },\n        {\n          label: \"Contact Us\",\n          href: routes.contact,\n          description: \"Reach out for personalised guidance\",\n        },\n      ],\n    },\n  ] as MenuColumn[],\n\n  /** Default feature box — shown when no item is hovered */\n  resourcesFeatureBox: {\n    icon: \"newspaper\",\n    title: \"Latest insights\",\n    description: \"Practical compliance updates and registration walkthroughs.\",\n    ctaLabel: \"Browse all resources\",\n    ctaHref: routes.resources,\n  } as FeatureBox,\n\n  /** Dynamic feature box content — changes based on hovered item.\n   *  Key = item href, Value = FeatureBox to display.\n   *  When an item is hovered, the feature box fades to its mapped content.\n   *  When no item is hovered, falls back to resourcesFeatureBox. */\n  resourcesHoverFeatureMap: {\n    [routes.blog]: {\n      icon: \"newspaper\",\n      title: \"Latest insights\",\n      description: \"Practical guides and compliance updates.\",\n      ctaLabel: \"Read the blog\",\n      ctaHref: routes.blog,\n    },\n    [routes.faq]: {\n      icon: \"help-circle\",\n      title: \"Common questions\",\n      description: \"Quick answers to registration and compliance topics.\",\n      ctaLabel: \"View all FAQs\",\n      ctaHref: routes.faq,\n    },\n    [routes.support]: {\n      icon: \"headset\",\n      title: \"Get help\",\n      description: \"Connect with the Exxonim team for guidance.\",\n      ctaLabel: \"Go to support\",\n      ctaHref: routes.support,\n    },\n    [routes.contact]: {\n      icon: \"mail\",\n      title: \"Reach out\",\n      description: \"Personalised guidance for your business routes.contact,\n  } as MenuFooterCta,\n\n  /** Links rendered after the dropdown menus (right side of nav pill) */\n  rightLinks: [\n    { label: \"Career\", href: routes.career },\n    { label: \"Contact\", href: routes.contact },\n  ] as MenuItem[],\n\n  /**\n   * Prominent highlighted link — Track Consultation is Exxonim's CORE DIFFERENTIATOR.\n   *\n   * This is not just another service — No other consultancy\n   * advertises proactive client updates or application tracking.\n   *\n   * How it works:\n   *   - Every engagement gets a unique tracking number (e.g., EXX-24091).\n   *   - At every milestone, the system sends proactive updates via WhatsApp,\n   *     email, or SMS — the client chooses their preferred channel.\n   *   - The client never needs to call to ask \"What's happening?\"\n   *   - No login, no dashboard — just enter a tracking number on the website\n   *     for an instant status lookup.\n   *\n   * BACKEND: If the admin renames or re-points this link in the future,\n   * update the label and href here. The visual treatment (accent pill + dot)\n   * is hardcoded in DesktopNavigation.tsx and MobileNavigationPanel.tsx.\n   */\n  highlightLink: {\n    label: \"Track Consultation\",\n    href: routes.trackConsultation,\n  } as MenuItem,\n};\n","import { useEffect, type RefObject } from \"react\";\n\nfunction getFocusableElements(node: HTMLElement) {\n  return Array.from(\n    node.querySelectorAll<HTMLElement>(\n      'a[href], button:not([disabled]), [tabindex]:not([tabindex=\"-1\"])'\n    )\n  ).filter((element) => !element.hasAttribute(\"disabled\"));\n}\n\nexport function useMobileMenuFocusTrap(\n  isOpen: boolean,\n  panelRef: RefObject<HTMLDivElement>,\n  fallbackRef: RefObject<HTMLButtonElement>\n) {\n  useEffect(() => {\n    if (!isOpen) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const panel = panelRef.current;\n    const previousActiveElement =\n      document.activeElement instanceof HTMLElement\n        ? document.activeElement\n        : null;\n\n    if (!panel) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    const focusableElements = getFocusableElements(panel);\n    const firstElement = focusableElements[0];\n    const lastElement = focusableElements[focusableElements.length - 1];\n\n    if (firstElement) {\n      firstElement.focus();\n    } else {\n      panel.focus();\n    }\n\n    const handleKeyDown = (event: KeyboardEvent) => {\n      if (event.key !== \"Tab\") {\n        return;\n      }\n\n      if (!focusableElements.length) {\n        event.preventDefault();\n        panel.focus();\n        return;\n      }\n\n      if (event.shiftKey && document.activeElement === firstElement) {\n        event.preventDefault();\n        lastElement.focus();\n      } else if (!event.shiftKey && document.activeElement === lastElement) {\n        event.preventDefault();\n        firstElement.focus();\n      }\n    };\n\n    document.addEventListener(\"keydown\", handleKeyDown);\n\n    return () => {\n      document.removeEventListener(\"keydown\", handleKeyDown);\n\n      if (\n        previousActiveElement &&\n        typeof previousActiveElement.focus === \"function\"\n      ) {\n        previousActiveElement.focus();\n      } else {\n        fallbackRef.current?.focus();\n      }\n    };\n  }, [fallbackRef, isOpen, panelRef]);\n}\n","import { useEffect, useId, useRef, useState, type FocusEvent } from \"react\";\nimport { Phone } from \"lucide-react\";\nimport { DesktopNavigation } from \"@/exxonim/components/navigation/DesktopNavigation\";\nimport { MobileNavigationPanel } from \"@/exxonim/components/navigation/MobileNavigationPanel\";\nimport { ThemeToggle } from \"@/exxonim/components/navigation/ThemeToggle\";\nimport { staticNav } from \"@/exxonim/content/staticNavigation\";\nimport { fallbackBrand } from \"@/exxonim/content/fallbackShell\";\nimport type { MenuKey } from \"@/exxonim/components/navigation/types\";\nimport { useMobileMenuFocusTrap } from \"@/exxonim/components/navigation/useMobileMenuFocusTrap\";\nimport { normalizePathname, routes } from \"@/exxonim/routes\";\nimport type { BrandAssets, CompanyInfo, Theme } from '@/exxonim/types';\nimport { cn } from \"@/exxonim/utils/cn\";\n\n/**\n * Navigation component — the fixed site header.\n *\n * ARCHITECTURE DECISION:\n * ──────────────────────\n * Navigation is now STATIC. It imports its structure from staticNavigation.ts\n * instead of fetching from the /navigation API. This was an intentional decision\n * because navigation items rarely change and should not depend on the backend.\n *\n * To add/remove/reorder navigation items, edit:\n *   src/exxonim/content/staticNavigation.ts\n *\n * The previous API-driven approach (navigationService.ts, usePublicShell navigation\n * query) is deprecated but kept in the codebase for reference if dynamic nav is\n * needed in the future.\n *\n * BACKEND / ADMIN INTEGRATION NOTES (FastAPI + PostgreSQL):\n * ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n * 1. `brand` (BrandAssets) — comes from site-settings API (key: \"brand\").\n *    Admin should allow uploading:\n *      - Light logo: SVG or PNG, min 140×36px, max 280×72px, aspect ratio ~3.9:1.\n *      - Dark logo: same dimensions. Must be legible on dark (#071518) backgrounds.\n *    The admin UI should validate aspect ratio and show a preview on both\n *    light and dark backgrounds before saving.\n *\n * 2. `company` (CompanyInfo) — comes from site-settings API (key: \"company_info\").\n *    Admin should validate:\n *      - phones[]: E.164 format preferred (e.g., \"+255 747 633 497\").\n *        Spaces in display format are OK — components strip them for tel: hrefs.\n *      - emails[]: valid email format.\n *      - whatsapp: full WhatsApp link (https://wa.me/255747633497).\n *        NOT just a phone number — must be a complete wa.me URL.\n *    These fields power the \"Call Now\" CTA and contact links in nav + footer.\n *\n * 3. Navigation structure is defined in staticNavigation.ts (see notes there).\n *    The admin does NOT manage navigation via the API in the current architecture.\n *\n * 4. Logo display uses `.logo-light` / `.logo-dark` CSS classes with opacity\n *    `@custom-variant dark` defined in globals.css. The logo <img> elements\n *    have an `onError` fallback that loads `fallbackBrand` assets when the\n *    API-sourced image fails to load.\n *\n * MOBILE NAVBAR — FLOATING PILL PATTERN (Mobbin-inspired):\n * ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n * On mobile (< xl), the header is a floating frosted-glass pill with 24px side\n * margins and 8px top offset, instead of a full-width bar. When the menu opens,\n * the pill expands downward in-place to reveal navigation links — no separate\n * overlay or side drawer. The frosted glass effect (blur(48px) + semi-transparent\n * background) keeps content visible but blurred behind the pill.\n */\ninterface NavigationProps {\n  brand: BrandAssets;\n  company: CompanyInfo;\n  pathname: string;\n  theme: Theme;\n  onToggleTheme: () => void;\n}\n\nfunction getHrefPath(href: string) {\n  return normalizePathname(href.split(\"#\")[0]);\n}\n\n/** Pages that are children of the \"Resources\" nav dropdown.\n *  When the user is on any of these paths, the Resources nav\n *  item should appear active — not Home or nothing. */\nconst RESOURCE_CHILD_PATHS = new Set([\n  normalizePathname(routes.faq),\n  normalizePathname(routes.support),\n  normalizePathname(routes.terms),\n  normalizePathname(routes.privacy),\n  normalizePathname(routes.cookies),\n  normalizePathname(routes.dataRights),\n  normalizePathname(routes.blog),\n]);\n\n/** Check if a path is a resource page or a resource child page.\n *  Matches: /resources, /blog, /faq, /support, /terms, /privacy,\n *  /cookies, /data-rights, and any /resources/{slug} article. */\nfunction isResourcesPath(path: string): boolean {\n  if (path === normalizePathname(routes.resources)) return true;\n  if (RESOURCE_CHILD_PATHS.has(path)) return true;\n  // Blog article paths: /resources/{slug} or /blog/{slug}\n  const segments = path.split(\"/\").filter(Boolean);\n  if (segments.length === 2 && (segments[0] === \"resources\" || segments[0] === \"blog\")) {\n    return true;\n  }\n  return false;\n}\n\n/* ── Morphing hamburger icon ────────────────────────────\n * Two horizontal lines that rotate ±45° to form an X.\n * CSS-driven animation via .nav-hamburger-open class.\n * Matches the Mobbin morph pattern — no icon swap. */\nfunction HamburgerIcon({ open }: { open: boolean }) {\n  return (\n    <span className=\"nav-hamburger relative w-5 h-5 flex items-center justify-center\">\n      <span\n        className={cn(\n          \"nav-hamburger-line absolute block w-[18px] h-[1.5px] rounded-full transition-transform duration-300 ease-[cubic-bezier(0.25,0.4,0.25,1)]\",\n          open ? \"rotate-45\" : \"rotate-0\"\n        )}\n      />\n      <span\n        className={cn(\n          \"nav-hamburger-line absolute block w-[18px] h-[1.5px] rounded-full transition-transform duration-300 ease-[cubic-bezier(0.25,0.4,0.25,1)]\",\n          open ? \"-rotate-45\" : \"rotate-0 translate-y-[5px]\"\n        )}\n      />\n    </span>\n  );\n}\n\nexport function Navigation({\n  brand,\n  company,\n  pathname,\n  theme,\n  onToggleTheme,\n}: NavigationProps) {\n  const brandName = company.name?.trim() || brand.name;\n  const mobilePanelRef = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\n  const mobileToggleRef = useRef<HTMLButtonElement>(null);\n  const servicesMenuId = useId();\n  const resourcesMenuId = useId();\n  const mobileMenuId = useId();\n\n  const currentPath = normalizePathname(pathname);\n  const [desktopMenu, setDesktopMenu] = useState<MenuKey | null>(null);\n  const [mobileMenuOpen, setMobileMenuOpen] = useState(false);\n\n  /* ── Scroll-based header transparency ──────────────────\n   * When at the top of the home page (over the hero), the\n   * header is transparent so the hero bleeds through.\n   * When scrolled past the hero, it gains a solid background.\n   * Only applies on the home page where a hero section exists.\n   *\n   * For the floating pill: transparency is subtle — the pill always\n   * has its frosted glass, but over the hero it's more transparent\n   * (lower bg opacity) and when scrolled it becomes more opaque. */\n  const isHomePage = currentPath === normalizePathname(routes.home);\n  const [scrolled, setScrolled] = useState(false);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    let frame = 0;\n    const updateScrolledState = () => {\n      frame = 0;\n      const nextScrolled = window.scrollY > 10;\n      setScrolled((previous) => (previous === nextScrolled ? previous : nextScrolled));\n    };\n\n    const handleScroll = () => {\n      if (frame) return;\n      frame = requestAnimationFrame(updateScrolledState);\n    };\n\n    updateScrolledState();\n    window.addEventListener(\"scroll\", handleScroll, { passive: true });\n    return () => {\n      window.removeEventListener(\"scroll\", handleScroll);\n      if (frame) cancelAnimationFrame(frame);\n    };\n  }, []);\n\n  /** True when the header should be transparent (over hero, at scroll top).\n   *  Sets data-over-hero attribute on <header> so CSS can handle logo\n   *  visibility WITHOUT React — preventing flash on page refresh.\n   *  CSS uses header[data-over-hero] + html[data-theme] to pick the right logo. */\n  const headerOverHero = isHomePage && !scrolled;\n  const primaryPhone = company.phones[0];\n  const callHref = primaryPhone\n    ? `tel:${primaryPhone.replace(/\\s+/g, \"\")}`\n    : routes.contact;\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    document.body.classList.toggle(\"overflow-hidden\", mobileMenuOpen);\n\n    return () => {\n      document.body.classList.remove(\"overflow-hidden\");\n    };\n  }, [mobileMenuOpen]);\n\n  useMobileMenuFocusTrap(mobileMenuOpen, mobilePanelRef, mobileToggleRef);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const handleViewportChange = () => {\n      if (window.innerWidth >= 1280) {\n        setMobileMenuOpen(false);\n      }\n      setDesktopMenu(null);\n    };\n\n    window.addEventListener(\"resize\", handleViewportChange);\n    window.addEventListener(\"orientationchange\", handleViewportChange);\n\n    return () => {\n      window.removeEventListener(\"resize\", handleViewportChange);\n      window.removeEventListener(\"orientationchange\", handleViewportChange);\n    };\n  }, []);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    const handleKeyDown = (event: KeyboardEvent) => {\n      if (event.key === \"Escape\") {\n        setDesktopMenu(null);\n        setMobileMenuOpen(false);\n      }\n    };\n\n    document.addEventListener(\"keydown\", handleKeyDown);\n    return () => document.removeEventListener(\"keydown\", handleKeyDown);\n  }, []);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    setDesktopMenu(null);\n    setMobileMenuOpen(false);\n  }, [pathname]);\n\n  const isActive = (href: string) => getHrefPath(href) === currentPath;\n  const closeAllMenus = () => {\n    setDesktopMenu(null);\n    setMobileMenuOpen(false);\n  };\n  const handleDropdownBlur = (event: FocusEvent<HTMLDivElement>) => {\n    const nextTarget = event.relatedTarget;\n    if (nextTarget instanceof Node && event.currentTarget.contains(nextTarget)) {\n      return;\n    }\n    setDesktopMenu(null);\n  };\n\n  return (\n    <>\n      {/* ── Mobile: Floating frosted pill (< xl) ────────────\n       * A detached capsule with generous margins, strong blur,\n       * and semi-transparent background. When the menu opens,\n       * this pill expands downward to reveal the navigation.\n       * Over hero: more transparent. Scrolled: more opaque. */}\n      <header\n        data-over-hero={headerOverHero ? \"\" : undefined}\n        className={cn(\n          \"xl:hidden fixed z-50 top-2 left-6 right-6\",\n          \"rounded-[28px]\",\n          \"transition-[background-color,backdrop-filter,opacity] duration-300\",\n          \"nav-pill\"\n        )}\n        style={{\n          backdropFilter: \"blur(48px)\",\n          WebkitBackdropFilter: \"blur(48px)\",\n        }}\n      >\n        {/* ── Pill bar (always visible) ── */}\n        <div className={cn(\n          \"flex items-center justify-between gap-3\",\n          \"px-5 py-3\",\n        )}>\n          {/* Logo */}\n          <a\n            href={routes.home}\n            onClick={closeAllMenus}\n            aria-label={`${brand.name} home`}\n            className=\"relative flex items-center min-w-0\"\n          >\n            <img\n              src={brand.lightLogoSrc}\n              alt={brand.name}\n              width=\"176\"\n              height=\"44\"\n              onError={(event) => {\n                const img = event.currentTarget;\n                if (img.dataset.fallbackApplied) return;\n                img.dataset.fallbackApplied = \"true\";\n                img.src = fallbackBrand.lightLogoSrc;\n              }}\n              className=\"logo-light block h-7 w-auto\"\n            />\n            <img\n              src={brand.darkLogoSrc}\n              alt=\"\"\n              aria-hidden=\"true\"\n              width=\"176\"\n              height=\"44\"\n              onError={(event) => {\n                const img = event.currentTarget;\n                if (img.dataset.fallbackApplied) return;\n                img.dataset.fallbackApplied = \"true\";\n                img.src = fallbackBrand.darkLogoSrc;\n              }}\n              className=\"logo-dark h-7 w-auto\"\n            />\n          </a>\n\n          {/* Hamburger/X morph */}\n          <button\n            ref={mobileToggleRef}\n            type=\"button\"\n            aria-label={mobileMenuOpen ? \"Close navigation\" : \"Open navigation\"}\n            aria-expanded={mobileMenuOpen}\n            aria-controls={mobileMenuId}\n            onClick={() => {\n              setDesktopMenu(null);\n              setMobileMenuOpen((open) => !open);\n            }}\n            className={cn(\n              \"flex items-center justify-center w-9 h-9 -mr-1\",\n              \"rounded-full transition-colors duration-200\",\n              \"hover:bg-accent-soft/40\",\n              headerOverHero\n                ? \"text-white dark-header-icon\"\n                : \"text-text\"\n            )}\n          >\n            <HamburgerIcon open={mobileMenuOpen} />\n          </button>\n        </div>\n\n        {/* ── Expandable menu (inside the pill) ── */}\n        <MobileNavigationPanel\n          brandName={brandName}\n          callHref={callHref}\n          currentPath={currentPath}\n          regularLinks={[...staticNav.leftLinks, ...staticNav.rightLinks]}\n          highlightLink={staticNav.highlightLink}\n          id={mobileMenuId}\n          isOpen={mobileMenuOpen}\n          resourcesActive={isResourcesPath(currentPath)}\n          servicesActive={currentPath === normalizePathname(routes.services)}\n          resourcesColumns={staticNav.resourcesColumns}\n          servicesColumns={staticNav.servicesColumns}\n          panelRef={mobilePanelRef}\n          primaryPhone={primaryPhone}\n          isActive={isActive}\n          onClose={() => setMobileMenuOpen(false)}\n          theme={theme}\n          onToggleTheme={onToggleTheme}\n        />\n      </header>\n\n      {/* ── Desktop: Full-width bar (xl+) ──────────────────\n       * Standard header bar with centered nav, Call CTA,\n       * theme toggle. Same as before — only the mobile\n       * shell changed to a floating pill. */}\n      <header\n        data-over-hero={headerOverHero ? \"\" : undefined}\n        className={cn(\n          \"hidden   : \"bg-page/95 backdrop-blur-xl\"\n        )}\n      >\n        <div className=\"h-full w-full px-[clamp(12px,2vw,24px)] grid xl:grid-cols-[1fr_auto_1fr] items-center justify-between xl:justify-center gap-4\">\n          {/* Left: Brand logo */}\n          <a\n            href={routes.home}\n            onClick={closeAllMenus}\n            aria-label={`${brand.name} home`}\n            className=\"relative flex items-center min-w-0\"\n          >\n            <img\n              src={brand.lightLogoSrc}\n              alt={brand.name}\n              width=\"176\"\n img.dataset.fallbackApplied = \"true\";\n                img.src = fallbackBrand.lightLogoSrc;\n              }}\n              className=\"logo-light block h-11 w-auto\"\n            />\n            <img\n              src={brand.darkLogoSrc}\n              alt=\"\"\n              aria-hidden=\"true\"\n              width=\"176\"\n              height=\"44\"\n              onError={(event) => {\n <DesktopNavigation\n            brandName={brandName}\n            leftLinks={staticNav.leftLinks}\n            rightLinks={staticNav.rightLinks}\n            highlightLink={staticNav.highlightLink}\n button.\n * Stacked directly above the WhatsApp button (same right position).\n * Same size as WhatsApp button (44×44px).\n * Only appears after the user scrolls down 400px.\n */\nexport function ScrollToTopButton() {\n  const [visible, setVisible] = useState(false);\n\n  useEffect(() => {\n    let frame = 0;\n\n    function updateVisibility() {\n      frame = 0;\n      const nextVisible = window.scrollY > 400;\n      setVisible((previous) => (previous === nextVisible ? previous : nextVisible));\n    }\n\n    function onScroll() {\n      if (frame) return;\n      frame = Button } from \"./primitives/Button\";\n\ninterface ErrorBoundaryProps {\n  children: ReactNode;\n  fallback?: ReactNode;\n}\n\ninterface ErrorBoundaryState {\n  hasError: boolean;\n  error: Error | null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Catches render errors in child components and displays a fallback UI\n * instead of crashing the entire application to a white screen.\n */\nexport class ErrorBoundary extends Component<ErrorBoundaryProps, ErrorBoundaryState> {\n  constructor(props: ErrorBoundaryProps) {\n    super(props);\n    this.state = { hasError: false, error: null };\n  }\n\n  static getDerivedStateFromError(error: Error): ErrorBoundaryState {\n    return { hasError: true, error };\n  }\n\n  componentDidCatch(error: Error, errorInfo: ErrorInfo) {\n    if (\n      typeof console !== \"undefined\" &&\n      typeof console.error === \"function\"\n    ) {\n      console.error(\"[ErrorBoundary] Uncaught render error:\", error, errorInfo);\n    }\n  }\n\n  handleReset = () => {\n    this.setState({ hasError: false, error: null });\n  };\n\n  handleReload = () => {\n    window.location.reload();\n  };\n\n  render() {\n    if (this.state.hasError) {\n      if (this.props.fallback) {\n        return this.props.fallback;\n      }\n\n      return (\n        <div\n          className={cn(\n            \"c-error-boundary\",\n            // Layout\n            \"flex items-center justify-center min-h-[60vh] px-6\",\n            // Visuals\n            \"bg-page text-text\"\n          )}\n        >\n          <div className=\"max-w-lg text-center\">\n            <div\n              className={cn(\n                // Layout\n                \"inline-flex items-center justify-center mb-6\",\n                // Sizing\n                \"w-16 h-16 fill=\"none\"\n                stroke=\"currentColor\"\n                strokeWidth={1.5}\n                strokeLinecap=\"round\"\n                strokeLinejoin=\"round\"\n </svg>\n            </div>\n\n            <h1 className=\"text-2xl font-bold text-text mb-3\">\n              Something went wrong\n            </h1>\n            <p className=\"text-text-muted You can try\n              again or reload the site.\n            </p>\n\n            {this.state.error && typeof this.state.error.message === \"string\" ? (\n              <pre className=\"mb-6 p-4 rounded-xl border border-border-soft bg-surface-soft text-xs text-text-muted text-left overflow-x-auto max-h-32\">\n                {this.state.error.message}\n              </pre>\n            ) : null}\n\n            <div className=\"flex flex-wrap justify-center gap-3\">\n              <Button\n                size=\"standard\"\n                variant=\"secondary\"\n                onClick={this.handleReset}\n              >\n                Try again\n              </Button>\n              <Button\n                size=\"standard\"\n                variant=\"primary\"\n                onClick={this.handleReload}\n              >\n                Reload page\n              </Button>\n            </div>\n          </div>\n        </div>\n      );\n    }\n\n    return this.props.children;\n  }\n}\n","const PUBLIC_CONTENT_CACHE_PREFIX = \"exxonim-public-content\";\nconst MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES = 50;\n\nexport type PublicContentSource = \"live\" | \"cache\" | \"fallback\";\n\ntype CachedPublicContentEnvelope<T> = {\n  version: 1;\n  cachedAt: string;\n  expiresAt: string | null;\n  value: T;\n};\n\nexport interface PublicContentState<T> {\n  data: T | undefined;\n  source: PublicContentSource;\n  isDegraded: boolean;\n  isStale: boolean;\n  cachedAt?: string;\n  expiresAt?: string | null;\n  error?: unknown;\n}\n\ninterface PublicContentCacheOptions<T> {\n  fallbackValue?: T;\n  ttlMs?: number;\n}\n\ninterface PublicContentFetchOptions<T> extends PublicContentCacheOptions<T> {\n  cacheKey: string;\n  fetcher: () => Promise<T>;\n  validate?: (value: T) => boolean;\n  warningLabel?: string;\n}\n\nfunction hasLocalStorage() {\n  return typeof window !== \"undefined\" && typeof window.localStorage !== \"undefined\";\n}\n\nfunction toStorageKey(cacheKey: string) {\n  return `${PUBLIC_CONTENT_CACHE_PREFIX}:${cacheKey}`;\n}\n\nfunction resolveExpiry(ttlMs?: number) {\n  if (!ttlMs || ttlMs <= 0) {\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  return new Date(Date.now() + ttlMs).toISOString();\n}\n\nfunction readCachedEnvelope<T>(cacheKey: string): CachedPublicContentEnvelope<T> | null {\n  if (!hasLocalStorage()) {\n    return null;\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const rawValue = window.localStorage.getItem(toStorageKey(cacheKey));\n    if (!rawValue) {\n      return null;\n    }\n\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(rawValue) as\n      | CachedPublicContentEnvelope<T>\n      | {\n          cachedAt?: string;\n          expiresAt?: string | null;\n          value?: T;\n        };\n\n    if (!parsed || typeof parsed !== \"object\" || !(\"value\" in parsed)) {\n      return null;\n    }\n\n    return {\n      version: 1,\n      cachedAt:\n        typeof parsed.cachedAt === \"string\"\n          ? parsed.cachedAt\n          : new Date().toISOString(),\n      expiresAt:\n        typeof parsed.expiresAt === \"string\" || parsed.expiresAt === null\n          ? parsed.expiresAt\n          : null,\n      value: parsed.value as T,\n    };\n  } catch {\n    return null;\n  }\n}\n\nexport function getCachedPublicContentState<T>(\n  cacheKey: string,\n  options: PublicContentCacheOptions<T> = {}\n): PublicContentState<T> {\n  const { fallbackValue } = options;\n  const cached = readCachedEnvelope<T>(cacheKey);\n\n  if (cached) {\n    const isStale = Boolean(\n      cached.expiresAt && new Date(cached.expiresAt).getTime() <= Date.now()\n    );\n\n    return {\n      data: cached.value,\n      source: \"cache\",\n      isDegraded: true,\n      isStale,\n      cachedAt: cached.cachedAt,\n      expiresAt: cached.expiresAt,\n    };\n  }\n\n  return {\n    data: fallbackValue,\n    source: \"fallback\",\n    isDegraded: typeof fallbackValue !== \"undefined\",\n    isStale: true,\n  };\n}\n\nexport function getCachedPublicContent<T>(\n  cacheKey: string,\n  fallbackValue?: T\n): T | undefined {\n  return getCachedPublicContentState<T>(cacheKey, { fallbackValue }).data;\n}\n\n/**\n * Scans all `exxonim-public-content:*` keys in localStorage, finds the entry\n * with the oldest `cachedAt` timestamp, and removes it.\n */\nfunction evictOldestCacheEntry() {\n  if (!hasLocalStorage()) {\n    return;\n  }\n\n  try {\n    let oldestKey: string | null = null;\n    let oldestTime = Infinity;\n\n    for (let i = 0; i < window.localStorage.length; i++) {\n      const key = window.localStorage.key(i);\n      if (!key || !key.startsWith(PUBLIC_CONTENT_CACHE_PREFIX + \":\")) {\n        continue;\n      }\n\n      try {\n        const raw = window.localStorage.getItem(key);\n        if (!raw) continue;\n        const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);\n        const cachedAt = typeof parsed.cachedAt === \"string\" ? parsed.cachedAt : undefined;\n        if (cachedAt) {\n          const time = new Date(cachedAt).getTime();\n          if (time < oldestTime) {\n            oldestTime = time;\n            oldestKey = key;\n          }\n        } else {\n          // No cachedAt – treat as infinitely old so it gets evicted first.\n          oldestKey = key;\n          oldestTime = -Infinity;\n        }\n      } catch {\n        // Malformed entry – evict it immediately.\n        oldestKey = key;\n        oldestTime = -Infinity;\n      }\n    }\n\n    if (oldestKey) {\n      window.localStorage.removeItem(oldestKey);\n    }\n  } catch {\n    // Ignore eviction failures.\n  }\n}\n\nfunction countCacheEntries(): number {\n  if (!hasLocalStorage()) {\n    return 0;\n  }\n\n  let count = 0;\n  for (let i = 0; i < window.localStorage.length; i++) {\n    const key = window.localStorage.key(i);\n    if (key && key.startsWith(PUBLIC_CONTENT_CACHE_PREFIX + \":\")) {\n      count++;\n    }\n  }\n  return count;\n}\n\nexport function cachePublicContent<T>(\n  cacheKey: string,\n  value: T,\n  options: Pick<PublicContentCacheOptions<T>, \"ttlMs\"> = {}\n) {\n  if (!hasLocalStorage()) {\n    return value;\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const payload: CachedPublicContentEnvelope<T> = {\n      version: 1,\n      cachedAt: new Date().toISOString(),\n      expiresAt: resolveExpiry(options.ttlMs),\n      value,\n    };\n\n    window.localStorage.setItem(toStorageKey(cacheKey), JSON.stringify(payload));\n\n    // Evict oldest entries if we've exceeded the maximum cache size.\n    while (countCacheEntries() > MAX_CACHE_ENTRIES) {\n      evictOldestCacheEntry();\n    }\n  } catch {\n    // Ignore cache write failures so public rendering never hard-fails on storage.\n  }\n\n  return value;\n}\n\nfunction warnWithFallback(label: string, error: unknown) {\n  if (\n    typeof window !== \"undefined\" &&\n    typeof console !== \"undefined\" &&\n    typeof console.warn === \"function\"\n  ) {\n    console.warn(label, error);\n  }\n}\n\nexport async function fetchWithFallbackResource<T>(\n  options: PublicContentFetchOptions<T>\n): Promise<PublicContentState<T>> {\n  const { cacheKey, fallbackValue, fetcher, ttlMs, validate, warningLabel } = options;\n\n  try {\n    const value = await fetcher();\n    if (validate && !validate(value)) {\n      throw new Error(`Live public content for \"${cacheKey}\" failed validation.`);\n    }\n\n    const cachedValue = cachePublicContent(cacheKey, value, { ttlMs });\n    const cachedAt = new Date().toISOString();\n    const expiresAt = resolveExpiry(ttlMs);\n\n    return {\n      data: cachedValue,\n      source: \"live\",\n      isDegraded: false,\n      isStale: false,\n      cachedAt,\n      expiresAt,\n    };\n  } catch (error) {\n    const fallback = getCachedPublicContentState<T>(cacheKey, {\n      fallbackValue,\n      ttlMs,\n    });\n\n    if (typeof fallback.data !== \"undefined\") {\n      warnWithFallback(\n        warningLabel ?? `Using cached or default public content for ${cacheKey}.`,\n        error\n      );\n\n      return {\n        ...fallback,\n        error,\n      };\n    }\n\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n\nexport async function fetchWithFallback<T>(\n  options: PublicContentFetchOptions<T>\n): Promise<T> {\n  const resource = await fetchWithFallbackResource<T>(options);\n  return resource.data as T;\n}\n","import type {\n  BlogAuthor,\n  BlogCategory,\n  BlogPost,\n  NavigationItem,\n  PageRecord,\n  PricingPlan,\n  SiteSetting,\n  Testimonial,\n} from '@/exxonim/types';\nimport type {\n  ApiBlogAuthor,\n  ApiBlogCategory,\n  ApiBlogPost,\n  ApiNavigationItem,\n  ApiPage,\n  ApiPricingPlan,\n  ApiSiteSetting,\n  ApiTestimonial,\n} from \"@/exxonim/types/api\";\n\nfunction toDateOnly(value?: string | null) {\n  if (!value) {\n    return \"\";\n  }\n\n  return new Date(value).toISOString().slice(0, 10);\n}\n\nexport function mapBlogAuthor(author?: ApiBlogAuthor | null): BlogAuthor | undefined {\n  if (!author) {\n    return undefined;\n  }\n\n  return {\n    id: author.slug,\n    name: author.name,\n    role: author.role ?? undefined,\n    avatarSrc: author.avatar_src ?? undefined,\n    bio: author.bio ?? undefined,\n  };\n}\n\nexport function mapBlogCategory(\n  category?: ApiBlogCategory | null\n): BlogCategory | undefined {\n  if (!category) {\n    return undefined;\n  }\n\n  return {\n    id: category.slug,\n    label: category.name,\n    description: category.description ?? undefined,\n  };\n}\n\nexport function mapBlogPost(post: ApiBlogPost): BlogPost {\n  return {\n    id: post.id,\n    slug: post.slug,\n    title: post.title,\n    excerpt: post.excerpt ?? \"\",\n    publishedAt: toDateOnly(post.published_at),\n    category: mapBlogCategory(post.category),\n    author: mapBlogAuthor(post.author),\n    coverImageSrc: post.featured_image ?? undefined,\n    coverAlt: post.cover_alt ?? undefined,\n    mediaLabel: post.media_label ?? post.title,\n    featuredSlot: post.featured_slot ?? undefined,\n    featuredOnHome: post.featured_on_home,\n    readTimeMinutes: post.read_time_minutes ?? undefined,\n    relatedSlugs: post.related_slugs ?? Used when the live FastAPI backend is unavailable.)\n *\n * Static fallback files are generated at build time by scripts/fetchFallbackAssets.mjs,\n * which fetches the latest API content and writes it to public/fallback/<key>.json.\n *\n * Supported keys:\n *   testimonials   → GET /api/v1/testimonials\n *   pages          → GET /api/v1/pages\n *   pricing        → GET /api/v1/pricing/plans\n *   navigation     → GET /api/v1/navigation\n *   blog-posts     → GET /api/v1/blog/posts\n *   blog-categories → GET /api/v1/blog/categories\n *   jobs           → GET /api/v1/jobs\n *   site-settings-brand → GET /api/v1/site-settings/brand\n *   site-settings-company-info → GET /api/v1/site-settings/company_info\n *   site-settings-footer → GET /api/v1/site-settings/footer\n *\n * PostgreSQL Tables:\n *   (Indirect — see the corresponding service files for table references)\n */\n\n/* ── Module-level cache ──────────────────────────────── */\n// Once a static fallback file has been fetched successfully, its parsed value\n// is held here so subsequent reads are synchronous.\nconst loaded: Map<string, unknown> = new Map();\n\n/* ── Eager preload map ────────────────────────────────── */\n// Services register their key + a promise here at module-init time. The\n// fetch starts immediately (while the app hydrates) so the value is ready\n// by the time a fallback is actually needed.\nconst preloads: Map<string, Promise<unknown>> = new Map();\n\n/**\n * Register an eager preload for a static fallback file.\n * Call this at module top-level (not inside a component or effect).\n */\nexport function preloadStaticFallback<T>(key: string): Promise<T | undefined> {\n  const existing = preloads.get(key);\n  if (existing) return existing as Promise<T | undefined>;\n\n  const promise = doLoadStaticFallback<T>(key).then((val) => {\n    if (typeof val !== \"undefined\") {\n      loaded.set(key, val);\n    }\n    return val;\n  });\n\n  preloads.set(key, promise);\n  return promise;\n}\n\n/**\n * Get a previously loaded static fallback value synchronously.\n * Returns undefined if the file hasn't finished loading or doesn't exist.\n */\nexport function getStaticFallback<T>(key: string): T | undefined {\n  return loaded.get(key) as T | undefined;\n}\n\n/**\n * Load a static fallback file, with an optional timeout.\n * This is the core fetch — it's called by `preloadStaticFallback` and can\n * also be called directly if eager preload isn't used.\n */\nasync function doLoadStaticFallback<T>(\n  key: string,\n  timeoutMs = 4_000\n): Promise<T | undefined> {\n  // Already in module cache\n  if (loaded.has(key)) {\n    return loaded.get(key) as T;\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const controller = new AbortController();\n    const timer = setTimeout(() => controller.abort(), timeoutMs);\n    const response = await fetch(`/fallback/${key}.json`, {\n      signal: controller.signal,\n      headers: { Accept: \"application/json\" },\n    });\n    clearTimeout(timer);\n\n    if (!response.ok) {\n      // 404 means the build script didn't generate this file — not an error\n      if (response.status === 404) return undefined;\n      return undefined;\n    }\n\n    const data = (await response.json()) as T;\n    loaded.set(key, data);\n    return data;\n  } catch {\n    // Network error, timeout, or invalid JSON — return undefined so the\n    // caller falls back to the hardcoded TypeScript default.\n    return undefined;\n  }\n}\n","import type {\n  AboutPageContent,\n  BlogCategory,\n  BlogPost,\n  CareerPageContent,\n  ContactPageContent,\n  FaqPageContent,\n  HomePageContent,\n  InfoPageContent,\n Centered subject (displayed as 38px circle).\n */\nexport const fallbackBlogPosts: BlogPost[] = [\n  {\n    id: 1,\n    slug: \"company-registration-basics\",\n    title: \"Company registration basics before your first filing\",\n    excerpt:\n      \"A short checklist for preparing incorporation details, supporting documents, and first-step authority submissions.\",\n    publishedAt: \"2026-03-22\",\n    category: getFallbackCategory(\"business-setup\"),\n    author: {\n      id: \"author-exxonim-1\",\n      name: \"Exxonim Advisory\",\n      role: \"Registration Specialist\",\n      avatarSrc: undefined,\n      bio: \"Exxonim's advisory team provides structured guidance through entity registration and compliance workflows.\",\n    },\n    coverImageSrc: blogCoverRegistration,\n    coverAlt: undefined,\n    mediaLabel: \"Company registration basics\",\n    featuredSlot: \"hero\",\n    featuredOnHome: true,\n    readTimeMinutes: 9,\n    relatedSlugs: [\"tin-registration-checklist\", \"compliance-calendar-basics\"],\n    content: {\n      introduction:\n        \"Start with the exact entity type, ownership information, and document set you expect to file so the process does not stall later.\",\n      highlights: [\n        \"Confirm the legal structure first.\",\n        \"Prepare identity and address documents before filing.\",\n        \"Map which authority comes first in your sequence.\",\n        \"Tanzania registration process.\",\n      ],\n      html: `<p>Starting a company in Tanzania involves several sequential steps that, when followed correctly, lead to a smooth registration process. Whether you are registering a limited liability company, a branch office, or a business name, the foundational requirements remain largely the same.</p>\n\n<h2>Choose the right entity type</h2>\n<p>The first and most important decision is selecting the correct legal structure for your business. In Tanzania, the main entity types are:</p>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Private Limited Company</strong> — The most common structure for local and foreign investors. Requires at least one director and one shareholder. Liability is limited to share capital.</li>\n<li><strong>Public Limited Company</strong> — Suitable for larger operations planning to raise capital publicly. Requires at least three directors and seven shareholders.</li>\n<li><strong>Business Name Registration</strong> — For sole proprietors or partnerships operating under a trade name. Simpler registration, but no limited liability protection.</li>\n<li><strong>NGO Registration</strong> — For non-profit organisations. Registered through the Ministry of Health, Community Development, Gender, Elderly and Children or the Vice President's Office.</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Choosing the wrong entity type early on can cause delays later when you discover that your structure does not support the activities you planned. Take time to confirm the right fit before moving forward.</p>\n\n<h2>Gather your documents before filing</h2>\n<p>Document readiness is the single biggest factor in whether a registration proceeds smoothly or stalls. Before you approach any authority, make sure you have the following prepared:</p>\n<ol>\n<li>Copy of national ID or passport for each director and shareholder</li>\n<li>Passport-size photographs of each director</li>\n<li>Proof of registered office address (lease agreement or utility bill)</li>\n<li>Draft memorandum and articles of association</li>\n<li>Proposed company names (at least three alternatives, in order of preference)</li>\n<li>Share capital details and allocation among shareholders</li>\n</ol>\n<p>Missing even one document can mean a return trip to the authority. A simple filing checklist prevents these avoidable delays.</p>\n\n<blockquote><p>The most common reason registrations stall is not the authority processing time — it is incomplete document submissions. Prepare thoroughly before your first filing.</p></blockquote>\n\n<h2>Understand the filing sequence</h2>\n<p>Registration in Tanzania follows a specific sequence. Attempting to skip steps or file out of order almost always creates additional work:</p>\n<p><strong>Step 1: Name reservation</strong> — Submit your proposed names to BRELA (Business Registration and Licensing Agency). Name clearance typically takes 1 to 3 working days. Once approved, the name is reserved for 30 days.</p>\n<p><strong>Step 2: Document preparation</strong> — With a reserved name, prepare the memorandum and articles of association, Form 1A (application for incorporation), and all supporting documents.</p>\n<p><strong>Step 3: Incorporation filing</strong> — Submit the complete package to BRELA. Processing typically takes 5 to 10 working days, though this can vary based on workload and completeness of your submission.</p>\n<p><strong>Step 4: Certificate collection</strong> — Once approved, collect your Certificate of Incorporation. This document is required for every subsequent step, including TIN registration, bank account opening, and business license applications.</p>\n\n<h2>Common mistakes that delay registration</h2>\n<p>After guiding hundreds of registrations, the same issues come up repeatedly. Being aware of these in advance can save weeks of delay:</p>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Name conflicts</strong> — Choosing names that are too similar to existing companies. Always prepare multiple alternatives.</li>\n<li><strong>Inconsistent information</strong> — Names, addresses, and ID numbers must match exactly across all documents. Even minor discrepancies trigger queries.</li>\n<li><strong>Missing share capital details</strong> — The minimum share capital for a limited company must be stated clearly. Ambiguity here causes rejection.</li>\n<li><strong>Incorrect forms</strong> — Using outdated or wrong BRELA forms. Always verify the current form version before submission.</li>\n</ul>\n\n<h2>What happens after registration</h2>\n<p>Receiving your Certificate of Incorporation is the beginning, not the end. Immediately after registration, you need to address several follow-on requirements:</p>\n<ol>\n<li><strong>TIN Registration</strong> — Apply for a Tax Identification Number with TRA (Tanzania Revenue Authority). Required for all registered entities.</li>\n<li><strong>Business License</strong> — Apply through your local government authority or the Online Business Registration Portal (BRIS).</li>\n<li><strong>Bank Account</strong> — Open a corporate bank account using your certificate and TIN.</li>\n<li><strong>VAT Registration</strong> — If your projected turnover exceeds the threshold, register for VAT within 30 days.</li>\n<li><strong>Sector-specific permits</strong> — Depending on your industry, additional permits may be required (e.g., TCIA for construction, TFDA for food and pharmaceuticals).</li>\n</ol>\n<p>Each of these steps has its own document requirements and processing times. Planning them in sequence — rather than discovering them reactively — keeps your business operational on schedule.</p>\n\n<blockquote><p>A successful registration is one where every step after incorporation has already been planned before the certificate is issued. TRA does not accept partial submissions.</p>\n\n<h2>Common consistency issues</h2>\n<p>The most frequent cause of TIN application rejection is inconsistency between documents. Small mismatches create avoidable back-and-forth during review:</p>\n<ul>\n<li>Company name spelling differences between the certificate and the application form</li>\n<li>Director names that do not match across ID documents and company records</li>\n<li>Address formats that differ between the lease agreement and the TRA form</li>\n<li>TIN numbers for existing directors that are recorded incorrectly</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Double-check every field against your source documents before submission. A 15-minute review can prevent a two-week delay.</p>\n\n<blockquote><p>Consistency across documents is not optional — it is the difference between a one-visit registration and a multi-visit correction cycle.</p></blockquote>\n\n<h2>Online vs. in-person registration</h2>\n<p>TRA now supports both online and in-person TIN applications. Each method has its own considerations:</p>\n<p><strong>Online registration</strong> through the TRA portal is faster for straightforward cases. Upload scanned documents, fill in the forms, and receive your TIN electronically. Processing typically takes 2 to 5 working days.</p>\n<p><strong>In-person registration</strong> at your local TRA office is better for complex cases, such as entities with foreign directors or those requiring additional clarification. You can address queries immediately rather than waiting for online correspondence.</p>\n<p>Choose the method that matches your situation. For most standard registrations, the online process is efficient and sufficient.</p>\n\n<h2>After you receive your TIN</h2>\n<p>Once your TIN is issued, several follow-up obligations take effect immediately:</p>\n<ol>\n<li>Update your bank account records with the TIN</li>\n<li>Include the TIN on all official invoices and receipts</li>\n<li>File your first tax return by the applicable deadline (even if no tax is due)</li>\n<li>Register for VAT if your turnover exceeds the threshold</li>\n<li>Set up electronic filing access through the TRA portal</li>\n</ol>\n<p>A TIN is not a one-time registration — it creates an ongoing relationship with the tax authority. A structured approach to renewal management prevents these outcomes.</p>\n\n<h2>Types of licences that require renewal</h2>\n<p>Most businesses in Tanzania hold multiple licences, each with its own renewal cycle:</p>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Business Licence</strong> — Annual renewal through the local government authority or BRIS portal</li>\n<li><strong>Professional Licences</strong> — Sector-specific permits (e.g., engineering, legal, medical) with varying renewal periods</li>\n<li><strong>Import/Export Permits</strong> — Typically annual, managed through TRA and relevant sector authorities</li>\n<li><strong>Occupational Licences</strong> — Health and safety permits, fire safety certificates, environmental compliance permits</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Each licence has a different renewal authority, different document requirements, and different processing times. Tracking them in one place is essential.</p>\n\n<h2>Build a renewal calendar</h2>\n<p>The foundation of effective renewal management is a single, visible calendar that captures every licence, its expiry date, and the lead time required for renewal. A practical renewal calendar includes:</p>\n<ol>\n<li><strong>Licence name and issuing authority</strong> — So you know where to go</li>\n<li><strong>Expiry date</strong> — The hard deadline</li>\n<li><strong>Internal reminder date</strong> — 60 days before expiry to begin preparation</li>\n<li><strong>Document checklist</strong> — What you need to submit</li>\n<li><strong>Assigned owner</strong> — One person accountable for tracking each renewal</li>\n</ol>\n<p>A shared filing calendar prevents deadlines from becoming last-minute recovery work. The calendar only helps if the team can see what is pending and who moves it forward.</p>\n\n<h2>Assign ownership per renewal stream</h2>\n<p>Without clear ownership, renewals fall through the cracks. Assign one person as the accountable owner for each renewal stream. Their responsibilities include:</p>\n<ul>\n<li>Monitoring the renewal calendar and triggering preparation at the reminder date</li>\n<li>Collecting and verifying all required documents before submission</li>\n<li>Following up with the issuing authority if processing takes longer than expected</li>\n<li>Confirming the renewal is completed and updating the calendar for the next cycle</li>\n</ul>\n<p>When multiple people share responsibility without clear accountability, everyone assumes someone else is handling it. One owner per stream eliminates this gap.</p>\n\n<blockquote><p>The cost of a missed renewal is always higher than the cost of preparation. Late fees, operational downtime, and reputational damage compound quickly.</p></blockquote>\n\n<h2>Common renewal pitfalls</h2>\n<p>After managing renewal cycles across dozens of organisations, the same patterns emerge:</p>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>Starting too late</strong> — 30 days is not enough for most renewals. Start at 60 days to accommodate document gathering and processing time.</li>\n<li><strong>Missing prerequisite updates</strong> — Some renewals require an up-to-date TIN certificate or tax clearance. If these have expired, the renewal itself will be delayed.</li>\n<li><strong>Ignoring processing delays</strong> — Authorities do not always process renewals on schedule. Build buffer into your timeline.</li>\n<li><strong>Not retaining proof</strong> — Always keep copies of submitted documents and receipts. If the authority loses your submission, you have evidence of timely filing.</li>\n</ul>\n\n<h2>When to seek professional support</h2>\n<p>For organisations with multiple licence types or those operating across different jurisdictions, professional compliance support can reduce risk and save time. The most effective calendars are simple, visible, and action-oriented — not comprehensive lists of every possible obligation buried in a spreadsheet nobody opens.</p>\n\n<h2>What makes a compliance calendar work</h2>\n<p>Three principles separate calendars that get used from calendars that get ignored:</p>\n<ol>\n<li><strong>Visibility</strong> — The calendar must be accessible to everyone who needs it, without requiring special software access or login credentials. A shared document or dashboard that updates in real time works best.</li>\n<li><strong>Action-orientation</strong> — Each entry should describe the next action, not just the deadline. \"Submit VAT return by March 25\" is more useful than \"VAT due Q1.\"</li>\n<li><strong>Single ownership</strong> — Every entry has one person responsible. If two people are listed, neither feels fully accountable.</li>\n</ol>\n<p>When these three elements are in place, the calendar becomes a working tool rather than a reference document.</p>\n\n<h2>Key dates to include</h2>\n<p>At minimum, your compliance calendar should capture the following recurring obligations:</p>\n<ul>\n<li><strong>VAT returns</strong> — Monthly or quarterly, depending on your registration status</li>\n<li><strong>PAYE remittances</strong> — Monthly, by the 9th of the following month</li>\n<li><strong>Corporate tax instalments</strong> — Quarterly, based on estimated annual tax</li>\n<li><strong>Business licence renewals</strong> — Annual, typically aligned with your registration anniversary</li>\n<li><strong>Annual return filing</strong> — Within 30 days of the company's anniversary of incorporation</li>\n<li><strong>Statutory audits</strong> — For companies meeting the turnover threshold, annually</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Each entry should include the authority, the document requirements, and the contact point for queries.</p>\n\n<blockquote><p>The calendar only helps if the team can see what is pending and who moves it forward. A hidden calendar is a useless calendar.</p></blockquote>\n\n<h2>Tools and formats</h2>\n<p>The format of your calendar matters less than whether people use it. Better for larger teams.</li>\n<li><strong>Dedicated compliance platform</strong> — Purpose-built tools with regulatory content pre-loaded. Suitable for organisations with complex compliance landscapes.</li>\n</ul>\n<p>Start with the simplest tool that your team will actually use. \"@/exxonim/types/api\";\nimport {\n  preloadStaticFallback,\n  getStaticFallback,\n} from \"./staticFallbackService\";\nimport { getFallbackSiteSetting } from \"@/exxonim/content/fallbackPublicContent\";\n\n// Eagerly load known site-setting fallback files at module init\npreloadStaticFallback<Record<string, SHELL_SITE_SETTING_TTL_MS = 1000 * 60 * 60 * 24;\n\nfunction siteSettingCacheKey(key: string) {\n  return `site-settings:${key}`;\n}\n\nfunction isSiteSettingRecord<TValue>(\n  value: SiteSetting<TValue> | undefined\n): value is SiteSetting<TValue> {\n  return Boolean(value && typeof value.key === \"string\");\n}\n\nasync function fetchFreshSiteSetting<TValue = unknown>(key: string) {\n  const response = await api.get<ApiSiteSetting<TValue>>(\n    apiRoutes.public.siteSettings.byKey(key)\n  );\n  return mapSiteSetting(response.data) as SiteSetting<TValue>;\n}\n\n/* ── Resolve a site-setting fallback ──────────────────────\n *  1. Check the static fallback JSON (build-time snapshot)\n *  2. Fall through to the hardcoded TypeScript default\n */\nfunction resolveFallbackSiteSetting<TValue = unknown>(\n  key: string\n): SiteSetting<TValue> | undefined {\n  const staticKey = `site-settings-${key}`;\n  const fromStatic = getStaticFallback<SiteSetting<TValue>>(staticKey);\n  if (fromStatic) return fromStatic;\n  return getFallbackSiteSetting(key) as SiteSetting<TValue> | undefined;\n}\n\nexport function getCachedSiteSetting<TValue = unknown>(key: string) {\n  return getCachedPublicContent<SiteSetting<TValue> | undefined>(\n    siteSettingCacheKey(key),\n    resolveFallbackSiteSetting<TValue>(key)\n  );\n}\n\nexport function getCachedSiteSettingResource<TValue = unknown>(key: string) {\n  return getCachedPublicContentState<SiteSetting<TValue> | undefined>(\n    siteSettingCacheKey(key),\n    {\n      fallbackValue: resolveFallbackSiteSetting<TValue>(key),\n      ttlMs: SHELL_SITE_SETTING_TTL_MS,\n    }\n  );\n}\n\nexport async function getSiteSetting<TValue = unknown>(key: string) {\n  return fetchWithFallback<SiteSetting<TValue>>({\n    cacheKey: siteSettingCacheKey(key),\n    fallbackValue: resolveFallbackSiteSetting<TValue>(key),\n    fetcher: () => fetchFreshSiteSetting<TValue>(key),\n    ttlMs: SHELL_SITE_SETTING_TTL_MS,\n    validate: isSiteSettingRecord,\n    warningLabel: `Using cached or default site setting for \"${key}\".`,\n  });\n}\n\nexport async function getSiteSettingResource<TValue = unknown>(key: string) {\n  return fetchWithFallbackResource<SiteSetting<TValue>>({\n    cacheKey: siteSettingCacheKey(key),\n    fallbackValue: resolveFallbackSiteSetting<TValue>(key),\n    fetcher: () => fetchFreshSiteSetting<TValue>(key),\n    ttlMs: SHELL_SITE_SETTING_TTL_MS,\n    validate: isSiteSettingRecord,\n    warningLabel: `Using cached or default site setting for \"${key}\".`,\n  });\n}\n","/**\n * FASTAPI ENDPOINT DEPENDENCIES:\n * ────────────────────────────────\n * GET /api/v1/site-settings/brand         — Get brand assets (public)\n * GET /api/v1/site-settings/footer        — Get footer content (public)\n * GET /api/v1/site-settings/company_info  — Get company info (public)\n *\n * See: src/exxonim/services/siteSettingsService.ts for full endpoint documentation.\n */\nimport { useQuery } from \"@tanstack/react-query\";\nimport {\n  getCachedSiteSettingResource,\n  getSiteSettingResource,\n} from \"@/exxonim/services/siteSettingsService\";\nimport type { SiteSettingFooterValue } from \"@/exxonim/types/api\";\nimport type { BrandAssets, CompanyInfo, SiteSetting } from '@/exxonim/types';\nimport {\n  fallbackBrand as defaultBrand,\n  fallbackCompanyInfo as defaultCompanyInfo,\n  fallbackFooter as defaultFooter,\n} from \"@/exxonim/content/fallbackShell\";\n\n/**\n * Shell data hook — loads brand, company info, and footer from the site-settings API.\n *\n * ARCHITECTURE NOTE:\n * ──────────────────\n * Navigation was previously loaded here via the /navigation API, but it has been\n * moved to a static configuration (staticNavigation.ts). The Navigation component\n * now imports its data directly instead of receiving it as a prop.\n *\n * The navigation query and navigationService.ts are deprecated but kept in the\n * codebase for reference. If API-driven navigation is needed in the future,\n * re-enable the navigation query here and pass navigationItems as a prop to\n * the Navigation component.\n */\n\ninterface PublicShellData {\n  brand: BrandAssets;\n  company: CompanyInfo;\n  footer: SiteSettingFooterValue;\n}\n\nfunction hasText(value: unknown): value is string {\n  return typeof value === \"string\" && value.trim().length > 0;\n}\n\nfunction sanitizeBrandAssets(value: BrandAssets): BrandAssets {\n  return {\n    name: hasText(value.name) ? value.name.trim() : defaultBrand.name,\n    lightLogoSrc: hasText(value.lightLogoSrc)\n      ? value.lightLogoSrc\n      : defaultBrand.lightLogoSrc,\n    darkLogoSrc: hasText(value.darkLogoSrc)\n      ? value.darkLogoSrc\n      : defaultBrand.darkLogoSrc,\n  };\n}\n\nfunction sanitizeCompanyInfo(value: CompanyInfo): CompanyInfo {\n  return {\n    ...value,\n    name: hasText(value.name) ? value.name.trim() : defaultCompanyInfo.name,\n  };\n}\n\nfunction hasSiteSettingValue<TValue>(\n  setting: SiteSetting<TValue> | undefined\n): setting is SiteSetting<TValue> {\n  return Boolean(setting?.value);\n}\n\nexport function usePublicShell(): PublicShellData {\n  const brandQuery = useQuery({\n    queryKey: [\"public-shell\", \"site-settings\", \"brand\"],\n    queryFn: () => getSiteSettingResource<BrandAssets>(\"brand\"),\n    initialData: () => getCachedSiteSettingResource<BrandAssets>(\"brand\"),\n    refetchOnMount: false,\n    refetchOnReconnect: \"always\",\n    refetchOnWindowFocus: false,\n    retry: false,\n    staleTime: 1000 * 60 * 60 * 4,\n  });\n  const footerQuery = useQuery({\n    queryKey: [\"public-shell\", \"site-settings\", \"footer\"],\n    queryFn: () => getSiteSettingResource<SiteSettingFooterValue>(\"footer\"),\n    initialData: () => getCachedSiteSettingResource<SiteSettingFooterValue>(\"footer\"),\n    refetchOnMount: false,\n    refetchOnReconnect: \"always\",\n    refetchOnWindowFocus: false,\n    retry: false,\n    staleTime: 1000 * 60 * 60 * 4,\n  });\n  const companyQuery = useQuery({\n    queryKey: [\"public-shell\", \"site-settings\", \"company_info\"],\n    queryFn: () =>
